rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DYKES VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint For Personal Injury And Property Damage
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 25, 2018 line 2. PLAINTIFF SCOTT DYKES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Second Amended Complaint For Personal Injury And Property Damage GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Court notes that Plaintiff again failed to comply with CRC 3.1324, specifically, 3.1324(a)(2)-(3), (b)(4). The Court also notes that per Notice Motion and Motion Defendant solely relies on CCP 473 and 576 and not on CCP 474 for the amendment. Motion is DENIED as to the proposed amendment to include Meridian Management Group, Inc., Lucerne Apartments, LP (and its partners/members/managers). Plaintiff failed to establish that involvement of these entities with the ownership/management of the premises or the identity of these entities were not known to Plaintiff prior to the expiration of the statute of limitations and/or at the time of the filing of the complaint. Plaintiff failed to show that the inclusion of these entities without reliance on "Doe" amendment will not be barred by the statue of limitations. The motion is GRANTED as to the inclusion of allegations regarding lead contamination. The Court is not making any rulings as to the application of the statute of limitations/relation back as to this allegation. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KLEIN VS. NEWELL & COMPANY, INC, DBA

rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ ET AL VS. LEANA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 25, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANTs LEANA PEREZ and FRANCISCO ARMAS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED. First Amended Complaint was filed without leave of Court and in violation of CCP 472. The operative complaint in this action remains the Complaint filed on August 10, 2017, subject to November 3, 2017 Order on Demurrer. The Peremptory Challenge to the Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay filed on November 28, 2017 has been denied on November 29, 2017 without prejudice as no proceeding was pending before said Judge at that time. This matter is not singly assigned. = (501/REQ) (late 3:19pm)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS L. VENTURONI ET AL VS. STEVEN MARTIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 25, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT STEVEN MARTIN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that the Notice on which this unlawful detainer is based was served in accordance with CCP 1162. If such allegation cannot be made in good faith, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that Notice based on CCP 1161(4) and SFRO 37.9(a)(3) may be served by certified mail as provided for "no cause" termination under CC 1946.1 used to prevent a renewal of the existing lease. Palmer v. Zeis (1944) 65 Cal.App.2d Supp.859. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. = (501/REQ)
